prompt: I'm running sp.process() in mongosh with a tumbling window pipeline over a Kafka topic but the window never closes and I see no output. Data is definitely flowing on the topic.

assertions.name: identifies_idle_partitions
assertions.description: Identifies idle Kafka partitions as the likely root cause — windows require ALL partitions to advance
assertions.reason: This is the most common cause of windowed processors producing no output when data is present

assertions.name: recommends_partitionIdleTimeout
assertions.description: Recommends adding partitionIdleTimeout to the $source stage

assertions.name: shows_corrected_pipeline
assertions.description: Shows corrected sp.process() call with partitionIdleTimeout in $source

root_cause: Idle Kafka partitions blocking window closure
explanation: Tumbling windows require all partitions to advance past the window boundary before emitting. If any partition has no new data, the global watermark stalls and the window never closes.

solution.explanation: After 30s of silence on a partition, treat it as idle and allow watermark to advance

common_mistakes.mistake: Suggesting the topic has no data
common_mistakes.error: User confirmed data is flowing — this is the wrong diagnosis
common_mistakes.fix: The issue is partition-level idleness, not topic-level emptiness

common_mistakes.error: User confirmed data flows without the window — connections are fine
common_mistakes.fix: Focus on the windowing mechanism, not connectivity
